episode_summary: The gang discuss George Eliot’s alleged giant right hand and her family’s decades-long denials, the use of ox testicle cords in grandfather clock mechanisms, how the first “motel” got its name from a too-small sign, and a deer poacher sentenced to watch Bambi monthly in prison. Along the way they dive into clocks, hotels’ reputations, and odd judicial punishments.

facts.fact: My fact is, after the author George Elliott died, her family spent 50 years publicly denying that she had a massive right hand.
My fact is that some grandfather clocks used to use ox testicles for their pulleys.
Okay, my fact this week is that the first motel was meant to be called a motor hotel, but the sign was too small to fit all the letters on.
My fact is that a man found guilty of deer poaching has been sentenced to a year in prison and been ordered to watch Bambi once a month during that year.
